title: Australian Fiduciaries Limited — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court froze assets and appointed receivers to entities related to Australian Fiduciaries Limited (AFL), which operated managed investment schemes into which approximately $160 million was invested by around 600 retail investors, largely via self-managed superannuation funds. On 16 June 2026, Justice Downes granted ASIC leave to amend its originating process to seek the winding-up of eight corporate defendants under receivership and three registered managed investment schemes AFL operated, with a hearing listed for 8 October 2026. ASIC separately cancelled AFL's Australian financial services licence on 2 July 2026.
scope: Subject of Federal Court asset-freezing orders and receivership over related entities; ASIC is seeking the winding-up of eight corporate defendants and three registered managed investment schemes it operated; Approximately $160 million invested by around 600 retail investors, largely through self-managed superannuation funds, is involved; ASIC cancelled the entity's Australian financial services licence on 2 July 2026; Matter is ongoing, with a further Federal Court hearing listed for 8 October 2026
